customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety — Melanie is a self-initiated, returning customer who resolved her prior blocker (roof repair) and called back voluntarily. Her motivation is genuine. The risk is not customer reluctance; it is a potential product-expectation mismatch from the prior engagement and Randy's unverified confirmation.
unresolved_concerns: Melanie's description of the financing program ('you would be the owner, I didn't have to pay for anything, I buy the energy at a set price') was validated by Randy without verification — closer must confirm whether Ion currently offers a PPA or lease structure matching this description before the appointment.
Roof repair scope was not probed — closer should confirm repair type, current roof age, and whether the roof is fully solar-ready per Ion's qualification standards.
sensitive_topics_raised: Prior appointment that did not proceed due to roof condition — Melanie is a returning lead; the closer should acknowledge continuity without dwelling on the prior gap.

Financing/ownership structure expectation — Melanie has a specific program in mind from the prior engagement; if Ion's current offering differs, this must be addressed early in the closer conversation, not after the design is presented.
exact_promises_rep_made: Confirmation text with date and time will be sent after the call.
closer_should_know: Verify Ion's current financing options (PPA/lease availability) before the 4PM appointment — Melanie described a specific program structure from her prior engagement and Randy confirmed it without verification. If Ion's current offering differs from what Melanie remembers, the closer needs to reframe this at the top of the call, not after the design is on screen. Additionally, no button-up verbal commitment ('is that fair') was obtained — treat this as a show-risk appointment and consider a pre-call confirmation.
